name: Luna Fit
summary: Calm, practical resets for people living inside real workdays.
audience: Busy desk workers who feel excluded by intense fitness culture.
promise: One useful movement reset that fits the time and space available now.
differentiator: Constraint-first demonstrations with body-neutral language and visible modifications.

body.name: Avenir Next
body.sample: A useful reset should fit the day you actually have.

wordmark.avatar_guidance: Use a close, recognizable face crop without overlay text.

production_spaces.title: Desk Corner
production_spaces.purpose: The recurring workday location where a constraint and tiny reset are visible together.
production_spaces.continuity_notes: Keep the desk, chair, window direction, and camera height stable.

production_spaces.title: Living Room Wall
production_spaces.purpose: A clear full-body space for standing movements and visible modifications.
production_spaces.continuity_notes: Keep the wall uncluttered, floor marks stable, and camera near waist height.

signature_props.title: Reset Timer
signature_props.role: A recurring object that makes Luna’s two-minute promise visible without adding fitness-theater styling.
signature_props.continuity_notes: Use the same matte timer, neutral face, and unbranded casing across content.

framing: Full movement when instruction requires it

headlines: Use the chair you already have.
caption_starters: Choose the version that fits today.

pillars.name: Desk Resets
pillars.description: One visible action that fits between meetings.

pillars.name: Habit Cues
pillars.description: Attach movement to an existing workday moment.

pillars.name: Modifications
pillars.description: Make the action fit the person and room.

qa_notes.title: Accessibility
qa_notes.body: Use Workday Ink on Warm Paper for body copy and never rely on color alone.

qa_notes.title: Production Rule
qa_notes.body: Every frame needs one action, one hierarchy, and one clearly visible modification.

qa_notes.title: Source Rule
qa_notes.body: Use approved creator references first and keep generated copy out of image pixels.
